State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration
(916) 322-5660

Executive Legal
322-5901

Enforcement
322-6441

Conflict of Interest
322-6444

May 22, 1978

Mr. William Taylor
San Diego County Counsel
355 County Administration Center
San Diego, California 92101

Dear Mr. Taylor:

This letter is in response to your letter of April 6, 1978, and our
telephone conversation regarding the Mootamai and Pauma Municipal Water
Districts. As you know, Government Code Section 81005.2(j)1/ provides:

Statements of economic interests required by Chapter 7 shall
be filed as follows:
...
(j) Persons not mentioned above - one original with the
agency. If the report or statement is filed by the head of
an agency, a member of a board or commission not under a
department of state government, or a member of a board or
commission not under the jurisdiction of a local legislative
body, the agency shall make and retain a copy and forward
the original to the code reviewing body which shall be the
filing officer.
[emphasis added]

- You indicated to me that some water districts are under the juris-
diction of the Board of Supervisors and some are not. Those districts
that are not under the jurisdiction of the Board must file Statements of
Economic Interests with the District which makes a copy and forwards the
original to the code reviewing body, in this case the Board of Supervisors.
You asked whether districts that are under the jurisdiction of the Board
could also be required to file copies of Statements with the Board.

It is the opinion of our staff that Section 81005.2(j) establishes
a minimal course of action which must be followed by governmental entities
throughout the State. However, the Board of Supervisors may exercise a

1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
noted.

Mr. William Taylor
May 22, 1978
Page Two

more rigorous standard of review with respect to Statements filed by members
of boards and commissions under its jurisdiction, so long as such review is
not inconsistent with the purposes of the Political Reform Act. In the
instant case, we do not think it is inconsistent with the purposes of
the Act for the Board of Supervisors to act as the filing officer for boards
and commissions under its jurisdiction in addition to the boards and commis-
sions for which it is statutorily required to be the filing officer.

Our conclusion is bolstered by the fact that code reviewing bodies
have been given great discretion to determine what is an agency for
purpose of adopting a conflict of interest code. Thus, many Boards of
Supervisors have adopted a single conflict of interest code for all depart-
ments, boards and commissions in the county. In such cases, the Board
of Supervisors is the filing officer for all designated employees covered
by the county code. In the instant case, requiring Statements of all
county boards and commissions to be filed with the Board of Supervisors
will make the Statements more readily available for public inspection and
promote uniform administration of the Act with regard to boards and commis-
sions in San Diego County.

It is also important to note that this advice imposes no additional
burden on the designated employee with respect to the filing of his Statement
of Economic Interests. The designated employee continues to file his
Statement with his board or commission. The additional burden, to the
extent it exists, is on the administrative personnel of the board or
commission to make and retain a copy and forward the original to the Board
of Supervisors.

Lastly, if the San Diego Board of Supervisors wishes to undertake such
a policy, it may do so by a policy statement or resolution. The codes of
local boards and commissions need not be amended unless they contain provi-
sions directly contradictory to such a policy. In passing, we note that if
the Board of Supervisors designates itself as the filing officer for boards
and commissions under its jurisdiction, it will undertake the ultimate
responsibility for reviewing all Statements, issuing late fines and refer-
ring potential violations to the civil and criminal prosecutors.

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact
me.

Sincerely,

Ted Prim
Chief, Conflicts of
Interest Division

TP:mfa

Conflict of Interest Div.

County of San Diego

OFFICE OF
COUNTY COUNSEL

355 COUNTY ADMINISTRATION CENTER
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101
(714) 236-3651

RONALD L. CLARK
County Counsel

JOSEPH KASE, JR.
Assistant County Counsel

April 6, 1978

Board of Directors
Moctamai Municipal Water District
Star Route, Box 155
Pauma Valley, California 92061

Honorable Board:

The Clerk of the Board of Supervisors has advised us that
her office has not to this date received the statements of economic interests filed pursuant to your District's Conflict of
Interest Code. This letter is to encourage your Board to forward
these statements to the Clerk immediately.

While the initial place of filing these statements is with
your District, the original statements must be forwarded to the
Clerk of the Board of Supervisors as the code reviewing body.
As Government Code Section 81005.2(j) states:

"If the report or statement is filed by . . . a
member of a board or commission not under a department
of state government . . . the agency shall make and
retain a copy and forward the original to the code
reviewing body which shall be the filing officer."

The time within which the agency is required to forward the
statements is clarified by Section 15115 of the regulations of
the Fair Political Practices Commission (Gov. Code, Title 2):

"Agencies which receive statements of economic
interest shall:

"(a) Within five days following the deadline for
filing such statements, forward each original statement
to the code reviewing body, indicating the date received
on the face of the statement, and retain a copy . . . ."

Board of Directors                    -2-              April 6, 1978
Mootamai Municipal Water District

     We are informed that the attorney for your District may
have possession of the original statements. In our opinion,
delivery of the statements to the agency's attorney for later
forwarding to the code reviewing body is not sufficient compliance with Section B1005.2(j) above. It is the District's duty
to see that the original statements are, in fact, forwarded to
the code reviewing body within the time specified. It should
also be pointed out that at all times after their filing with the
agency (the District), the statements must be made available to
the public for inspection as a public record (Gov. Code, § 81008).

     We trust you will take immediate steps to comply with the
above requirements. Thank you for your cooperation.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        DONALD L. CLARK, County Counsel

                                        By  [illegible]
                                            WILLIAM T. TAYLOR, Deputy

WWT:am

cc  San Diego County District Attorney
    Attention Mr. James Hamilton
    Fair Political Practices Commission
    Conflict of Interest Division
    Mr. Don F. Shefto, Clayson, Rothrock & Mann
    Clerk of Board of Supervisors

CONFLICT OF INTEREST DIVISION

County of San Diego

OFFICE OF
COUNTY COUNSEL
550 COUNTY ADMINISTRATION CENTER
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101
(714) 236-5661

April 6, 1978

DONALD L. CLARK
County Counsel

JOSEPH KASE, JR.
Assistant County Counsel

Board of Directors
Pauma Municipal Water District
c/o Millard J. Beemer
Star Route, Box 104
Pauma Valley, California 92061

Honorable Board:

The Clerk of the Board of Supervisors has advised us that
her office has not to date received the statements of economic interests filed pursuant to your District's Conflict of
Interest Code. This letter is to encourage your Board to forward
these statements to that Clerk immediately.

While the initial place of filing these statements is with
your District, the original statements must be forwarded to the
Clerk of the Board of Supervisors as the code reviewing body.
As Government Code Section 81005.2(j) states:

"If the report or statement is filed by . . .
a member of a board or commission not under a department
of state government, . . . the agency shall make and
retain a copy and forward the original to the code
reviewing body which shall be the filing officer."

The time within which the agency is required to forward the
statements is clarified by Section 18115 of the regulations of
the Fair Political Practices Commission (Gov. Code, Title 2):

"Agencies which receive statements of economic
interest shall:

"(a) Within five days following the deadline for
filing such statements, forward each original statement
to the code reviewing body, indicating the date received
on the face of the statement, and retain a copy; . . ."

Board of Directors
Pauma Municipal Water District

-2-

April 6, 1978

We are informed that the attorney for your District may
have possession of the original statements. In our opinion,
delivery of the statements to the agency's attorney for later
forwarding to the code reviewing body is not sufficient compliance with Section 81005.2(j) above. It is the District's duty
to see that the original statements are, in fact, forwarded to
the code reviewing body within the time specified. It should
also be pointed out that at all times after their filing with the
agency (the District), the statements must be made available to
the public for inspection as a public record (Gov. Code, § 81008).

We trust you will take immediate steps to comply with the
above requirements. Thank you for your cooperation.

Sincerely,

DONALD L. CLARK, County Counsel

By [illegible] W. Taylor
WILLIAM W. TAYLOR, Deputy

   Attention Mr. James Hamilton

   Fair Political Practices Commission
   Conflicts of Interest Division

   Mr. Don F. Shefto, Clayson, Rothrock & Mann

   Clerk of Board of Supervisors
